_readme: Form-check exercise library. Each exercise carries plain-language cues (always shown) and, when autoCheck=true, declarative RULES the rule engine can evaluate from 2D pose landmarks. Cues were synthesised in our own words from reputable, clinician- or coach-authored sources (see each exercise's `sources`). This is a thoroughly specified STARTER set covering the reliably pose-checkable core movements; extend toward 100 using the same schema and sourcing rules described in the Claude Code prompt. Do NOT paste verbatim transcript text into this file.
landmarks: Semantic names map to MediaPipe Pose 33-landmark indices in code: nose=0, L/R shoulder=11/12, L/R elbow=13/14, L/R wrist=15/16, L/R hip=23/24, L/R knee=25/26, L/R ankle=27/28, L/R heel=29/30, L/R footIndex=31/32.

metricVocabulary.jointAngle: Angle (deg) at vertex B for points [A,B,C]. e.g. knee = [hip,knee,ankle].
metricVocabulary.segmentVsVertical: Angle (deg) of segment [A,B] from true vertical. 0 = perfectly upright.
metricVocabulary.segmentVsHorizontal: Angle (deg) of segment [A,B] from horizontal. 0 = perfectly level.
metricVocabulary.horizontalRatio: Absolute horizontal distance |x_a - x_b|, normalised by `normalize` length (usually torso = shoulder->hip). Camera-distance invariant.
metricVocabulary.verticalRatio: Absolute vertical distance |y_a - y_b|, normalised by `normalize` length.
metricVocabulary.frontSeparationRatio: Front view only. horizontalDistance(p1a,p1b) / horizontalDistance(p2a,p2b). Used for knee valgus: kneeGap/ankleGap.
metricVocabulary.leadRate: Compares how fast point A rises vs point B during a phase. Positive = A moved up faster than B (used to catch 'hips shoot up first').

exercises.name: Bodyweight Squat
cues: Set feet about shoulder width with toes turned out slightly (roughly 11 and 1 o'clock).
Keep your weight spread across the whole foot — heel, midfoot and ball — rather than rocking onto heels or toes.
Start the descent by sending the hips back and down at the same time, not by shooting the knees forward.
Track the knees out over the toes; don't let them collapse inward.
Keep a braced core and a neutral spine — chest stays up, low back doesn't round or over-arch.
Drive up by pushing the floor away; let hips and shoulders rise together.

rules.name: Insufficient depth
rules.cue: Aim for thighs closer to parallel if mobility allows — but quality beats forcing depth.

rules.name: Knees caving inward
rules.cue: Think 'spread the floor' — drive the knees out to track over the toes.

rules.name: Hips rise faster than shoulders
rules.cue: Drive hips and chest up together — if the hips shoot up first the load dumps onto your lower back.

rules.name: Excessive forward lean / rounding
rules.cue: Keep the chest up and brace harder; a big forward tip usually means the core gave way.

sources.name: Squat University (Dr. Aaron Horschig, PT, DPT, CSCS)

exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat
cues: Pin the bar tightly to the shelf of your upper back; build a stable tripod foot.
Take a big breath and brace the trunk hard before you unrack and before each rep.
Initiate with a hip hinge to load the posterior chain, keeping the bar over your midfoot the whole way.
Generate external-rotation torque at the hips — cue 'squeeze the glutes / knees out to the toes'.
Stand up by driving the hips, not by letting them shoot up while the chest folds forward.
Better a slightly higher rep with clean alignment than a deep rep that rounds the back.

rules.name: Knees caving inward
rules.cue: Drive the knees out to track over the toes; strengthen glutes/hips for stability.

rules.name: Hips rise faster than shoulders
rules.cue: Keep hips and chest rising together; hips shooting up shifts load to the lower back.

rules.name: Lower back rounding at the bottom
rules.cue: Brace harder and stop just above the depth where your back loses its neutral arch.

sources.name: Squat University – How to Perfect the High-Bar Back Squat (Dr. Aaron Horschig, PT, DPT, CSCS)

exercises.name: Goblet Squat
cues: Hold the weight close to your chest with elbows tucked; this helps keep the torso upright.
Same rules as the bodyweight squat: hips back and down together, knees over toes, whole-foot contact.
Let the elbows pass inside the knees at the bottom without the back rounding.

rules.name: Knees caving inward
rules.cue: Drive the knees out over the toes.

rules.name: Excessive forward lean
rules.cue: Keep the weight against the chest and stay tall; a big lean means you're losing the brace.

sources.name: Squat University (Dr. Aaron Horschig, PT, DPT, CSCS)

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift (RDL)
cues: Soft knees only — a slight, fixed bend of about 15–25 degrees; this is a hinge, not a squat.
Push the hips straight back to lower the weight; the bar/dumbbells track close to the legs the whole way.
Keep a long, neutral spine — chest proud, lats engaged; don't let the upper back round as you descend.
Lower until you feel a hamstring stretch (usually mid-shin) without the back rounding, then drive the hips forward to stand.
Finish tall by squeezing the glutes; don't over-arch or lean back at the top.

rules.name: Squatting instead of hinging
rules.cue: Keep the knees softly fixed and send the hips back — if the knees keep bending you're turning it into a squat.

rules.name: Lower/upper back rounding
rules.cue: Stop the descent where your spine can stay neutral; brace the lats and keep the chest long.

rules.name: Weight drifting away from the legs
rules.cue: Keep the bar/dumbbells brushing close to the thighs and shins; drift adds load to the lower back.

rules.name: Hips rise before the bar
rules.cue: Drive hips forward and bring the chest up together rather than letting the hips outrun the torso.

sources.name: Synthesised from hip-hinge biomechanics guidance (Squat University / clinician-authored hinge tutorials)

exercises.name: Conventional Deadlift
cues: Set the bar over your midfoot; hips higher than the knees, shoulders just in front of the bar.
Take a big breath, brace, and set a neutral spine before the bar leaves the floor.
Push the floor away and keep the bar dragging close to your legs in a near-vertical path.
Hips and shoulders rise together off the floor — don't let the hips shoot up and turn it into a stiff-legged pull.
Lock out by standing tall and squeezing glutes; don't hyperextend or lean back.

rules.name: Back rounding at the start
rules.cue: Reset with a neutral spine and brace before pulling; lighten the load if you can't hold it.

rules.name: Hips shoot up first
rules.cue: Push the floor away so hips and chest rise together.

rules.name: Bar drifting forward
rules.cue: Keep the bar against your legs; engage the lats to pull it back toward you.

sources.name: Synthesised from clinician/coach deadlift tutorials (Squat University, Barbell Logic)

exercises.name: Forward Lunge
cues: Step out and lower straight down so the front thigh reaches roughly parallel and the front shin stays fairly vertical.
Keep the torso tall and core braced; avoid pitching the chest forward over the knee.
Track the front knee over the toes — don't let it cave inward.
Push back through the front heel to return; keep control rather than collapsing into the bottom.

rules.name: Shallow front-knee bend
rules.cue: Lower until the front thigh is about parallel, if your knee is pain-free.

rules.name: Front knee caving in
rules.cue: Drive the front knee out to stay stacked over the foot.

rules.name: Torso pitching forward
rules.cue: Stay tall through the torso and brace; leaning forward overloads the lower back.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
cues: Set the front foot far enough forward that the shin stays near vertical at the bottom.
Lower straight down with a tall, slightly forward-inclined torso and braced core.
Keep the front knee tracking over the toes; don't let it dive inward.
Drive through the front heel and the mid-foot to stand.

rules.name: Front knee caving in
rules.cue: Press the front knee out over the foot.

rules.name: Shallow depth
rules.cue: Aim for front thigh near parallel if pain-free.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
cues: Feet flat, heels fairly close to the hips; press through the heels.
Squeeze the glutes to lift the hips until shoulder–hip–knee form a straight line.
Stop at a neutral plank at the top — don't hyperextend the lower back to go higher.
Keep the rib cage down and core gently braced; the glutes do the work, not the low back.

rules.name: Over-arching at the top
rules.cue: Stop at a straight line; arching past neutral loads the lower back instead of the glutes.

rules.name: Hips not fully extending
rules.cue: Squeeze the glutes harder to bring the hips up to a neutral line.

exercises.name: Barbell Hip Thrust
cues: Upper back on the bench, chin tucked, ribs down; shins vertical at the top.
Drive through the heels and squeeze the glutes to a neutral, flat top position.
Stop at a straight torso-to-thigh line — don't arch the lower back to chase range.
Lower under control; keep a posterior pelvic tilt cue to keep the glutes loaded.

rules.name: Over-arching / ribs flaring at the top
rules.cue: Tuck the ribs and stop at a flat top; hyperextension turns this into a lower-back exercise.

rules.name: Incomplete hip lockout
rules.cue: Finish each rep with a full glute squeeze to a neutral line.

exercises.name: Wall Sit
cues: Slide down a wall until thighs are about parallel and knees are near 90 degrees.
Keep shins vertical (knees stacked over ankles) and the whole back flat against the wall.
Brace the core; hold without letting the hips creep up over time.

rules.name: Thighs not parallel
rules.cue: Adjust until the knees sit close to 90 degrees with thighs parallel to the floor.

exercises.name: Push-Up
cues: Hands roughly under the shoulders, wrists stacked under shoulders from the side view.
Keep the body in one straight line from shoulders through hips to ankles — no sagging hips or piked hips.
Let the elbows travel back at about a 30–45 degree angle from the torso, not flared straight out.
Lower until the chest is near the floor while keeping the body flat like a board.
Keep the neck neutral — eyes down and slightly ahead, don't crane the head to reach the floor.

rules.name: Hips sagging (low back extends)
rules.cue: Brace the core and squeeze the glutes to keep a straight plank line.

rules.name: Hips piked up
rules.cue: Lower the hips into line; piking shifts the work off the chest.

rules.name: Not lowering far enough
rules.cue: Lower until the elbows reach roughly 90 degrees / chest near the floor.

sources.name: Peak Endurance Physical Therapy – Proper Push-Up Form
House of Physical Therapy – Push-Up Form

exercises.name: Standing Overhead Press
cues: Bar/dumbbells start at the front of the shoulders; forearms vertical from the side view.
Squeeze the glutes and brace the abs hard before each rep to lock the pelvis and prevent back arch.
Press in a vertical line over the crown of the head; move the head back slightly so the bar passes the face, then bring the torso through.
Finish locked out with the weight stacked over the shoulders and midfoot; shrug slightly at the top.
Keep the knees and hips locked — no leg drive on a strict press.

rules.name: Excessive lower-back arch / leaning back
rules.cue: Squeeze the glutes and brace harder to keep the torso upright; if you still arch, the weight is too heavy.

rules.name: Not locking out overhead
rules.cue: Press all the way to a stacked lockout with the arms by the ears.

rules.name: Bending the knees (turning it into a push press)
rules.cue: Keep hips and knees locked on a strict press so the shoulders do the work.

sources.name: BarBend – Overhead Press Mistakes and Fixes

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
cues: Slight bend in the elbows held constant; raise from the shoulders, not by swinging.
Lift to about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor) — no higher is needed.
Lead with the elbows, keeping wrists from rising above them.
Control the lowering; avoid using momentum or a torso heave to start the rep.

rules.name: Raising well above shoulder height
rules.cue: Stop around shoulder height; going much higher shrugs the traps in.

rules.name: Swinging the torso for momentum
rules.cue: Keep the torso still; if you're rocking to lift, lighten the dumbbells.

exercises.name: Bent-Over Row
cues: Hinge to a flat, braced torso — roughly 15–45 degrees above horizontal — and hold that angle throughout.
Pull the elbows back and toward the ribs, bringing the weight to the lower ribs / belly.
Squeeze the shoulder blades back and down; avoid shrugging up toward the ears.
Don't heave with the lower back or stand up to move the weight — the torso angle stays fixed.

rules.name: Standing up / torso rising to lift
rules.cue: Lock the torso angle and move only the arms; if the torso rises with each rep, lighten the load.

rules.name: Back rounding under load
rules.cue: Reset to a neutral, braced spine before rowing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl
cues: Keep the upper arms pinned by your sides; the elbows stay roughly fixed in place.
Curl by bending only at the elbow; don't swing the weights up with the shoulders or torso.
Control the lowering through a full range to near-straight arms.
Keep the torso upright and still — no leaning back to heave the weight.

rules.name: Elbows swinging forward
rules.cue: Keep the elbows pinned at your sides; let the elbow joint do the work.

rules.name: Swinging the torso
rules.cue: Stand tall and still; if you're rocking to curl, lighten the weight.

rules.name: Short range of motion
rules.cue: Lower to near-straight arms each rep for full range.

exercises.name: Front Plank
cues: Elbows under the shoulders; body in one straight line from head to heels.
Brace the core and squeeze the glutes so the hips neither sag nor pike up.
Keep a neutral neck — gaze just ahead of the hands.
Breathe steadily; hold quality position rather than maximum time.

rules.name: Hips sagging
rules.cue: Lift the hips into line and brace; sagging stresses the lower back.

rules.name: Hips piked up
rules.cue: Drop the hips to a flat line so the core does the work.

exercises.name: Side Plank
cues: Elbow under the shoulder; stack the body in a straight diagonal line.
Lift the hips so the bottom hip doesn't drop toward the floor.
Keep the core braced and the neck neutral.

rules.name: Bottom hip dropping
rules.cue: Lift the hips to keep one straight diagonal line from head to feet.

exercises.name: Bird Dog
cues: From all fours, extend the opposite arm and leg to about parallel with the floor.
Keep the spine neutral and the hips level — don't rotate or arch the lower back.
Move slowly with control; brace the core throughout.
Return without letting the back sag, then switch sides.

rules.name: Lower back over-arching on extension
rules.cue: Keep the torso flat and level; lift the leg only to hip height, not higher.

exercises.name: Pull-Up
cues: Start from a full hang with active shoulders (slight shoulder-blade depression, not fully relaxed).
Pull the elbows down and back to bring the chin over the bar.
Avoid big leg kips/swings on a strict pull-up; keep the body controlled.
Lower under control to a full hang each rep.

rules.name: Chin not clearing the bar
rules.cue: Pull until the chin clears the bar for a full rep.

rules.name: Excessive swinging
rules.cue: Brace the core to reduce swing on a strict pull-up.

cueOnlyExamples: Many common exercises are valuable but hard to evaluate reliably with a single 2D camera (occlusion, lying positions, fine grip/bracing). Ship these as cue-only entries first (autoCheck=false, cues + sources, no rules): bench press, incline press, chest fly, triceps pushdown, skullcrusher, lat pulldown, seated cable row, face pull, shrug, calf raise, leg press, leg extension, leg curl, Russian twist, crunch, sit-up, mountain climber, burpee, kettlebell swing, farmer's carry, Romanian single-leg deadlift, Copenhagen plank, dead bug, hollow hold.
